function.name: mock_banking_get_accounts
function.description:
List the user's bank accounts with balances.

USE THIS TOOL WHEN: The user asks about their accounts, balances,
disposable amount, overdraft, wants to see all accounts, or refers
to an account by name or alias.

DO NOT USE WHEN: The user asks about a specific account's interest rates
or detailed terms -- use mock_banking_get_account_detail instead.

account_type.description: Filter accounts by type. 'all' returns every account. Example: 'CURRENT' for checking accounts, 'SAVING' for savings.

include_details.description: If true, return full account details including rates, flags, and sub-objects. If false, return summary with balance only.

search_text.description: Fuzzy search against account alias or product name. Handles partial matches. Example: 'vacation' matches 'Vacation Fund'. Use when the user refers to an account by name.

function.name: mock_banking_get_account_detail
function.description:
Get full details for a specific bank account including reservations.

USE THIS TOOL WHEN: The user asks about interest rates, overdraft terms,
loan details, saving goals, saving targets, pending transactions,
reserved amounts, or specific account features.

DO NOT USE WHEN: The user just wants to see their balance -- use
mock_banking_get_accounts instead.

account_id.description: Account ID to look up. Get this from a previous mock_banking_get_accounts call. Example: 'ACC001'

include_reservations.description: If true, include pending card reservations (authorized but not yet booked transactions). Shows merchant name, amount, date, and status.

function.name: mock_banking_get_cards
function.description:
List the user's payment cards.

USE THIS TOOL WHEN: The user asks about their cards, card status,
expiry dates, card types, card delivery status, or whether a card
is active/blocked.

DO NOT USE WHEN: The user asks about specific card limits or credit
card outstanding -- use mock_banking_get_card_detail instead.

card_type.description: Filter cards by type. 'all' returns every card. Example: 'CREDIT' for credit cards, 'BANK_CARD' for debit cards.

include_limits.description: If true, include ATM/POS/internet daily and monthly limits. If false, return card summary without limit details.

function.name: mock_banking_get_card_detail
function.description:
Get full details for a specific payment card.

USE THIS TOOL WHEN: The user asks about exact card limits (ATM, POS,
internet), temporary limits, credit card outstanding, installment due date,
minimum repayment, 3D Secure status, delivery mode, or specific features.

DO NOT USE WHEN: The user just wants to list their cards -- use
mock_banking_get_cards instead.

card_id.description: Card ID to look up. Get this from a previous mock_banking_get_cards call. Example: 'CARD001'

function.name: mock_banking_search_transactions
function.description:
Search and filter the user's transactions with fuzzy merchant matching.

USE THIS TOOL WHEN: The user asks about transactions, payments, spending
at a specific store, salary received, recent activity, "did I get paid?",
ATM withdrawals, standing orders, card transactions, or wants to exclude
own-account transfers from results.

DO NOT USE WHEN: The user asks for aggregated spending summaries or top
merchants -- use mock_banking_spending_summary instead.

IMPORTANT: Use EITHER search_text OR category, never both together.
"How much on food?" -> use category="FOOD" (not search_text).
"How much at Lidl?" -> use search_text="Lidl" (not category).

account_id.description: Account ID to search transactions for. Get this from mock_banking_get_accounts. None searches all accounts. Example: 'ACC001'

search_text.description: Fuzzy text search against merchant name, title, description, partner name. Handles abbreviations and variants (e.g. 'McD' matches 'McDonald\'s'). Example: 'McDonalds', 'Starbucks', 'Shell'

direction.description: Filter by direction. 'INCOMING' for received, 'OUTGOING' for paid.

category.description: Filter by spending category. Includes both expense AND income categories.

from_date.description: Start date filter in YYYY-MM-DD format.

to_date.description: End date filter in YYYY-MM-DD format.

card_number.description: Filter transactions by masked card number (e.g. '451161XXXXXX6044'). Use when the user asks about spending on a specific card.

transaction_type.description: Filter by transaction type. Use for standing orders, ATM withdrawals, etc.

exclude_own_transfers.description: Exclude transfers between user's own accounts. Critical for accurate spending totals. Set to False only when user explicitly asks about own transfers.

offset.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

function.name: mock_banking_spending_summary
function.description:
Get aggregated spending summary by category, merchant, week, or month.

USE THIS TOOL WHEN: The user asks "how much did I spend on food?",
"top merchants?", "monthly spending trend?", "spending by subcategory?",
or wants to compare spending across categories or time periods.

DO NOT USE WHEN: The user asks about specific transactions -- use
mock_banking_search_transactions instead.

account_id.description: Account ID. None includes all accounts. Example: 'ACC001'

group_by.description: How to group spending. 'category' = by main type (FOOD, CAR, etc.), 'subcategory' = detailed breakdown within categories, 'merchant' = by store/vendor (with fuzzy normalization), 'week' = ISO week, 'month' = YYYY-MM.

category.description: Filter to specific category before grouping. When group_by='subcategory', this scopes which main category to break down.

card_number.description: Filter spending to a specific card number before aggregation.

exclude_own_transfers.description: Exclude own transfers from spending totals.

top_n.description: Number of top groups to return (1-50).

function.name: mock_banking_get_budget_status
function.description:
Check budget usage and remaining amounts by spending category.

USE THIS TOOL WHEN: The user asks "how much budget left for food?",
"am I on track?", "budget status?", or mentions spending limits.

DO NOT USE WHEN: The user asks about actual transactions -- use
mock_banking_search_transactions instead.

category.description: Budget category to check. 'all' returns all budgets.

function.name: mock_banking_find_merchant
function.description:
Find merchant names that fuzzy-match a search query.

USE THIS TOOL WHEN: You need to disambiguate a merchant name before
searching transactions, or the user asks "what merchants match X?".

DO NOT USE WHEN: You want to search transactions directly --
use mock_banking_search_transactions(search_text=...)
which has built-in fuzzy matching.

query.description: Merchant name or abbreviation to search for. Example: 'McD', 'starbucks', 'albert'

limit.description: Maximum number of merchant matches to return (1-20).

function.name: knowledge_search
function.description: Search the knowledge base (groups: KB_GAI_SK_SK_2026-03-17_17h26) for information about bank products, services, policies, fees, eligibility, limits, features, app/portal instructions, and other institution-specific topics. Returns relevant knowledge documents with group name, summary, and description. Use this tool whenever the user asks about bank-specific information. Pass the user's message as-is into the question parameter. Optionally provide query variations in 'queries' and expected facets in 'expected_facets'.

question.description: The user's original question, passed verbatim. Do NOT rephrase, shorten, or extract keywords — pass the full user message exactly as stated.

queries.description: Optional query variations covering different angles of the question. If empty, the question itself is used as the single query.

expected_facets.description: Optional aspects the answer should cover (e.g. 'fees', 'eligibility').
